title: Pseudo Anonymised Pregnancies from Questionnaire R0
description: Variables from the pregnancies section of the first round of questionnaire for The Generations Study about the participant's history of being or trying to become pregnant, and their births. This data has been pseudo-anonymised by removing any personal identifiable information (PII) and shifting dates from their original data.

R0_InfertilityPeriod.x-question: Was there ever a time as long as a year or more when you tried to get pregnant, but did not?
R0_InfertilityPeriod.description: Participant experienced infertility for a year.

R0_InfertilityAge.x-question: If yes, how old were you when you tried to get pregnant but couldn't?

R0_InfertilityDiagnosis.x-question: Have you ever been told by a doctor that you or your partner had an infertility problem?
R0_InfertilityDiagnosis.description: Did the doctor diagnose the participant with infertility problems.

R0_InfertilityReason.x-question: If yes, what was the reason for the difficulty in conceiving?
x-enumDescriptions: 4: Partner had low sperm count or other problem.

R0_FertilityDrugs.x-question: Were you given fertility drugs or hormones to help you conceive?
R0_FertilityDrugs.description: Use of fertility drugs/hormones by the participant.

R0_FertilityTreatmentAgeFirst.x-question: At what age were you first treated?
R0_FertilityTreatmentAgeFirst.description: Age at participant's first fertility treatment.

R0_FertilityTreatmentAgeLast.x-question: At what age were you last treated?
R0_FertilityTreatmentAgeLast.description: Age at participant's last fertility treatment.

R0_EverPregnant.description: Whether or not the participant has ever been pregnant.

R0_TotalPregnancies.x-question: If you have ever been pregnant, how many pregnancies have you had in total?
R0_TotalPregnancies.description: The number of pregnancies the participant has had in total. Maximum is max number of pregnancies in cohort for baseline questionnaire.

R0_PregnanciesMoreThan6.x-question: If you have had more than 6 pregnancies, cross here.

Pregnancies.description: An array of pregnancy records (up to 13).

R0_Preg_Num.description: PregnancyID of the participant's pregnancy. Used to differentiate pregnancy data into correct variable naming conventions in post processing of this array.

R0_PregnancyEndDate_Shifted.name: R0_PregnancyEndDate_Shifted
R0_PregnancyEndDate_Shifted.description: Derived and pseudo-anonymised full date. Day, month and year components are combined and the resulting date is shifted by a participant-specific offset. If the day component is missing it is set to 15 before shifting. If the month component is missing (and a year is present) the date is treated as year-only, anchored at 1 July (day 1, month 7) of that year before shifting. If day and month are both missing the same rule applies. The resulting dates are displayed by the non-assumedlevel that was in the raw data, i.e. if they day was missing in the raw data it will not be shown in the aggregated date, and same for month. This preserved the number of days that dates are shifted by per particiapnt. If the year component is missing thederived value is null.

R0_Preg_Outcome.description: The outcome of the participant's pregnancy.

R0_Preg_DurationWks.description: Length in weeks of the participant's pregnancy. Maximum set at 54 as longest pregnancy recorded in UK was 375 days.

R0_Preg_SevereVomiting.x-question: Did you ever have severe vomiting in the first 3 months of pregnancy?
R0_Preg_SevereVomiting.description: Whether or not the participant suffered from severe vomiting in the first three months of their pregnancy.

R0_Preg_Eclampsia.x-question: Did you suffer from eclampsia or pre-eclampsia during pregnancy?
R0_Preg_Eclampsia.description: Whether or not the participant suffered from eclampsia or pre-eclampsia during their pregnancy.

R0_Preg_ChildSex.x-question: Sex of child from  pregnancy, if known
R0_Preg_ChildSex.description: The sex of the child, or children, from the participant's pregnancy.

R0_Preg_BirthWghtG.x-question: Birthweight of child from pregnancy, if known. Grams
R0_Preg_BirthWghtG.description: The birthweight of the child from the participant's pregnancy in grams. Maximum set to equivalent of grams for 15 pounds, 8 ounces.

R0_Preg_BirthWghtlbs.x-question: Birthweight of child from pregnancy, if known. Pounds
R0_Preg_BirthWghtlbs.description: The birthweight of the child from the participant's pregnancy in pounds.. Maximum set at 15 pounds as heaviest recorded birth in UK was 15 pounds, 8 ounces.

R0_Preg_BirthWghtOzs.x-question: Birthweight of child from pregnancy, if known. Ounces
R0_Preg_BirthWghtOzs.description: The birthweight of the child from the participant's pregnancy in ounces.

R0_Preg_BreastfeedingWks.x-question: Number of weeks breastfed after pregnancy.
R0_Preg_BreastfeedingWks.description: Number of weeks that the participant breastfed after their pregnancy.

R0_Preg_MilkSuppression.x-question: Did you receive pills or hormone injection to dry up your milk secretion after pregnancy?
R0_Preg_MilkSuppression.description: Whether or not the participant received pills or hormone injections to dry up their milk secretion after their pregnancy.

R0_BreastfeedingBreast.x-question: Which breast did you use for breastfeeding?
R0_BreastfeedingBreast.description: Which breast the participant used for breastfeeding.

R0_FirstPregWghtStone.x-question: What was your weight at the beginning of your first pregnancy? Stone.
R0_FirstPregWghtStone.description: The participant's weight in stone at the start of their first pregnancy. Maximum set at 48 as heaviest recorded woman UK was 48 stone.

R0_FirstPregWghtlbs.x-question: What was your weight at the beginning of your first pregnancy? Pounds.
R0_FirstPregWghtlbs.description: The participant's weight in pounds at the start of their first pregnancy. This variable works in conjunction with stone. Every increase of 14 pounds is another stone.

R0_FirstPregWghtKG.x-question: What was your weight at the beginning of your first pregnancy? Kilograms.
R0_FirstPregWghtKG.description: The participant's weight in kilograms at the start of their first pregnancy. Maximum set at 305 as heaviest recorded woman UK was 48 stone.

R0_LastPregWghtStone.x-question: What was your weight at the beginning of your last pregnancy? Stone.
R0_LastPregWghtStone.description: The participant's weight in stone at the start of their last pregnancy. Maximum set at 48 as heaviest recorded woman UK was 48 stone.

R0_LastPregWghtlbs.x-question: What was your weight at the beginning of your last pregnancy? Pounds.
R0_LastPregWghtlbs.description: The participant's weight in pounds at the start of their last pregnancy. This variable works in conjunction with stone. Every increase of 14 pounds is another stone.

R0_LastPregWghtKG.x-question: What was your weight at the beginning of your last pregnancy? Kilograms.
R0_LastPregWghtKG.description: The participant's weight in kilograms at the start of their last pregnancy. Maximum set at 305 as heaviest recorded woman UK was 48 stone.

R0_PregHrmns.x-question: Have you ever taken hormones to maintain a pregnancy?
R0_PregHrmns.description: Whether the participant took hormones to maintain a pregnancy.

R0_PregHrmnsAge.x-question: If yes, at what age were you treated with hormones during pregnancy?
R0_PregHrmnsAge.description: The participant's age when treated with hormones during pregnancy.
